meaning: n.基架,底座,基础vt.加座,搁在台上,支持
examples: I replaced the vase carefully on its pedestal.
Since childhood, I put my own parents on a pedestal. I felt they could do no wrong.
"I ask not wherefore, nor how, thou hast fallen into the pit, or say, rather, thou hast ascended to the pedestal of infamy, on which I found thee." He said.
Apple has been put on a pedestal and no one expects them to dominate anything.
It brings down the barriers that usually put journalists up on some omniscient pedestal.
